neighborhoods.name: Ghent
neighborhoods.description: Norfolk's most walkable urban village with locally owned shops, restaurants, and cafes along Colley Avenue and 21st Street. A mix of early-1900s row houses, apartments, and bungalows with a strong arts and dining scene.

housing.predominantType: Row houses, bungalows, and mid-rise apartment buildings from early 1900s

neighborhoods.character_notes: Ghent is the cultural heart of Norfolk. The Naro Expanded Cinema, Plot coffee, and dozens of independent restaurants line Colley Ave. Saturday farmers market at the Chrysler Museum. Very dog-friendly with sidewalk dining.

neighborhoods.name: Colonial Place
neighborhoods.description: A quiet, established residential neighborhood on a peninsula surrounded by the Lafayette River, with tree-lined streets, waterfront parks, and a strong sense of community.

housing.predominantType: Colonial and Cape Cod-style single-family homes, some duplexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: One of Norfolk's most desirable neighborhoods. The Colonial Place Civic League is very active. Waterfront lots along the Lafayette River offer kayaking and sunset views. Car recommended for errands.

neighborhoods.name: Freemason District
neighborhoods.description: Norfolk's oldest residential neighborhood featuring cobblestone streets, three centuries of architectural styles, and waterfront condos along the Elizabeth River. Walking distance to downtown and the Waterside District.
neighborhoods.character: Historic urban, upscale, walkable to downtown, waterfront

housing.predominantType: Historic row houses, converted warehouse lofts, waterfront condominiums

neighborhoods.character_notes: The most historic neighborhood in Norfolk, with homes dating to the 1700s. Walking distance to Town Point Park, Waterside District, and the Tide light rail. Popular with professionals and empty-nesters seeking walkable urban living.

neighborhoods.name: Larchmont-Edgewater
neighborhoods.description: A prestigious residential neighborhood west of Colonial Place near Old Dominion University, known for waterfront homes on the Lafayette River, top-rated schools, and a quiet suburban feel within city limits.

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes, Tudor and Colonial Revival styles, some waterfront estates

neighborhoods.character_notes: Home to ODU faculty, military officers, and professionals. The Larchmont Library is a community anchor. Beautiful tree canopy and the Larchmont Village shopping area offers a small-town feel. Car needed for most errands.

neighborhoods.name: Ocean View
neighborhoods.description: A revitalizing beachfront neighborhood along the Chesapeake Bay with new construction, affordable housing, and direct beach access. Norfolk's most beach-oriented community with a growing restaurant scene.

housing.predominantType: Mix of new construction townhouses, renovated cottages, and older apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Ocean View Beach Park and the Community Beach are the main draws. The East Beach development has brought upscale townhomes. Several new breweries and restaurants along Ocean View Ave. Active redevelopment with rising property values.

sources.title: CoVA Collective Realty - Best Neighborhoods in Norfolk
